creator: glennpj@charter.net
text:
On Fri, Sep 14, 2001 at 06:27:28AM -0700, Graham Lillico wrote:

> >Number:         30573
> >Category:       ports
> >Synopsis: /usr/X11R6/bin/xfce_setup does not create .xsession and
> >.Xclients files correctly
> >Confidential:   no
> >Severity:       non-critical
> >Priority:       low
> >Responsible:    freebsd-ports
> >State:          open
> >Quarter:        
> >Keywords:       
> >Date-Required:
> >Class:          sw-bug
> >Submitter-Id:   current-users
> >Arrival-Date:   Fri Sep 14 06:30:00 PDT 2001
> >Closed-Date:
> >Last-Modified:
> >Originator:     Graham Lillico
> >Release:        4.3-RELEASE
> >Organization:
> >Environment:
> FreeBSD freebsd1.home.org 4.3-RELEASE FreeBSD 4.3-RELEASE #0: Sat Apr 21 10:54:49 GMT 2001     jkh@narf.osd.bsdi.com:/usr/src/sys/compile/GENERIC  i386
> >Description:
> When running /usr/X11R6/bin/xfce_setup the .xsession and .Xclients
> files are created with #!/bin/bash, however bash is installed in
> /usr/local/bin, also xfce-3.8.6_1 does not require bash
> >How-To-Repeat:
> run /usr/X11R6/bin/xfce_setup 
> >Fix:
> edit /usr/X11R6/etc/xfce/xsession and /usr/X11R6/etc/xfce/Xclients
> replacing
> 
> #!/bin/bash 
> 
> with 
> 
> #!/usr/local/bin/bash
> >Release-Note:
> >Audit-Trail:
> >Unformatted:
> 
> To Unsubscribe: send mail to majordomo@FreeBSD.org
> with "unsubscribe freebsd-ports" in the body of the message

This would not be the correct fix.  You said, correctly, that xfce does
not require bash.  The correct fix would be to change '/bin/bash' with
'/bin/sh'

-- 
Glenn Johnson
glennpj@charter.net
